person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
In 1166 he was recorded as a knight of the Archbishop of York.

Thurstan was a royal servant. In 1171-2, after the death of Thomas Becket, he was one of the royal keepers of the Archbishopric of Canterbury while no successor to Becket had been appointed. He acted as a royal justice, helped to collect a royal aid (a form of taxation) in 1177, and, from 1177 to 1190, was Constable of Ludlow Castle, Shropshire with an annual allowance of £16 10s.

At one stage he was involved in a minor dispute with a member of the staff of Henry II's chancery: the official had not issued him with forma instructions of his duties as a royal official. It emerged that Thurstan had previously declined a request from the official for a gift of two royal cakes, and Thurstan was commanded to kneel before the official to beg for his instructions, with a gift of a cake.

«b»Parents and Lineage«/b»
Thurstan le Despencer was the son of Simon le Despencer.

The Complete Peerage states that this Thurstan was most probably the grandson of Hugh Dispenser and his wife Hawise.

«b»Property«/b»
Thurstan was the son of Simon Dispenser, inheriting his lands by 1153, when he was involved in a legal dissolute with the monks of Abingdon, Berkshire. His birth date is uncertain.

Among the lands he held were the manor of Rollright, Oxfordshire and estates in Gloucestershire and Worcestershire.

It is not certain that Thurstan held land at Charlecote in Warwickshire. His son Walter is recorded as Walter fitz Thurstan of Charlecote but that probably means that it was Walter who was of Charlecote, not Thurstan.

«b»Death«/b»
He may have died in about 1191, when he was involved in a legal case over his Worcestershire property, and when the final recorded payment of his allowance as Constable of Ludlow Castle was made.

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Walter

2.) Amaury (Almaric)

3.) Hugh

4.) Turstin

Thurstan probably had another son, Roger, who may have been illegitimate.

«b»Research Notes«/b»
The Spencer pedigree in The Visitation of Warwickshire for 1619 conflated several families together.

The chart shows Thurstan as the son of Hugh de Dutton.
This chart also shows him as the ancestor of Hugh le Despenser.
These relationships have been proven false, thus this pedigree chart should not be relied upon as a whole.
In addition, no part of this pedigree was published in the later Visitation of Warwick for 1682/3.
Lewis cites The Complete Peerage, but the information is about a different family: Reynold de Lucy who married Amabel, daughter of William FitzDuncan. Reynold's heir by Amabel, Richard de Lucy. Reynold died about January 1198/99. Richard married Ada, eldest daughter of Hugh de Morville. Richard died s.p.m. in 1213 and by 13 August 1218, Ada had married (2) Thomas de Multon. This family is a different branch of the Lucys than Geoffrey de Lucy who married Juliane le Despsenser.

From another profile of a Walter Despenser (Despencer-20), attached as a son of Thurston prior to 17 February 2023:

Between 1181 and 1189 Henry II granted the manor at Kings Worthy, Hampshire, to Walter, the usher of the king's chamber, to hold in chief by the annual rent of a pair of gilded spurs. Walter was succeeded by his brother Aimery le Despenser, who in 1205 obtained from King John a confirmation of "the land of Worthy which King Henry our father gave to Walter the son of Thurstan".

-------

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Thurstan
meanings: An old name. The same as the Old and Middle High German names Turstan, Thurstan, Turstein, signifying 'Thor's stone;' figuratively 'Thor's defence;' from Anglo-Saxon stdn, Old Norman steinn. It is found Latinised Turstanus. Thor is found in many old names, as Thurgar, Thurgood, Thurkettle, Thurkle, Thurtle.

en.lifeSummary: When Thurstan le Despenser was born in 1122, in London, England, United Kingdom, his father, Guillaume III Talvas, was 35 and his mother, Hélène de Bourgogne, was 42. He married Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire about 1146, in London, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. He died in 1209, in Rutland, England, at the age of 87, and was buried in Ryhall, Rutland, England.

Il a épousé Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire environ 1146, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Il est décédé en 1209, à Rutland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 87 ans, et a été enterré à Ryhall, Rutland, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Thurstan le Despenser(이)가 1122년 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillaume III Talvas(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Hélène de Bourgogne(은)는 42세였다. 그는 대략 1146년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1209년 영국 루틀랜드에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 루틀랜드 리홀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thurstan le Despenser nasceu em 1122, em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Guillaume III Talvas, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Hélène de Bourgogne, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire aproximadamente 1146, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1209, em Rutland, Inglaterra, com 87 anos, e foi sepultado em Ryhall, Rutland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thurstan le Despenser родился в 1122 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Guillaume III Talvas было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Hélène de Bourgogne, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire около 1146 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Он умер в 1209 г. в местности рутланд, Англия в возрасте 87 лет и был похоронен в местности рихолл, рутланд, Англия.

es.Neufchâtel-en-Saosnois, Sarthe, Pays de: Neufchâtel-en-Saosnois, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thurstan le Despenser nació en 1122, en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Guillaume III Talvas, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Hélène de Bourgogne, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire aproximadamente 1146, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. Murió en 1209, en Rutland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 87 años, y fue sepultado en Ryhall, Rutland, Inglaterra.
